item.title: Pride Month may be ending, but the work doesn't stop here.
item.description:
Pride Month may be ending, but the work doesn't stop here.
Throughout June, we talked about where Pride began, how it looks different around the world, and the local community keeping it alive in Darmstadt through groups like Vielbunt. That same spirit needs to carry into the other eleven months of the year too.
Being an ally isn't a one-time action, it's a daily practice, especially in the moments that don't make it onto a feed. A small comment left unchallenged, a colleague who doesn't feel safe being out, a friend who needs someone to actually listen, these are the everyday tests of allyship.
So as the rainbow flags come down this week, here's what staying engaged can look like:
Listen without judgment
Speak up against discrimination
Educate yourself
Share queer voices
Support LGBTQIA+ spaces and people
Let's show up, for each other, every day.

item.title: Looking for queer community in Darmstadt? Vielbunt is a vibrant local organis...
item.description:
Looking for queer community in Darmstadt? Vielbunt is a vibrant local organisation creating safe spaces, events, and visibility for LGBTQIA+ people in and around Darmstadt.
Whether you're looking for connection, support, or just want to get involved, they're a great place to start. Visibility matters, 365 days a year.
Get involved @vielbunt.org
CSD Darmstadt takes place on 15 August 2026!

item.title: Questions about your visa or residence permit? We've got you covered!
item.description:
Questions about your visa or residence permit? We've got you covered!
Our support sessions are available both in person and online. Visit our website for session times, the online link, and everything you need to prepare.

item.title: It's going to be very hot in Darmstadt this week. Please take care of yoursel...
item.description:
It's going to be very hot in Darmstadt this week. Please take care of yourself! Stay in the shade, drink plenty of water, and avoid physical exertion during the hottest parts of the day.
If you're heading to a lake or river to cool off, please take extra care. Hot weather can make swimming more risky than it seems. Natural bodies of water can have unexpected cold zones, strong currents, or sudden depth changes that can catch even confident swimmers off guard. If you didn't grow up swimming regularly, it's especially worth being cautious: go in slowly, stay close to the shore, and never swim alone.
Water Refill stations

item.title: Please note that Monday, 25 May 2026 is Whit Monday (Pfingstmontag), a public...
item.description:
Please note that Monday, 25 May 2026 is Whit Monday (Pfingstmontag), a public holiday in Germany.
TU Darmstadt will be closed, and most supermarkets, grocery stores, and shops will not be open. We recommend stocking up on everything you need this weekend.
Wishing everyone a relaxing long weekend!

item.title: Please note that the Health Insurance advisory session on Thursday, 21 May 20...
item.description:
Please note that the Health Insurance advisory session on Thursday, 21 May 2026 will not take place.
Please check our website for the next available session:
🔗 https://www.tu-darmstadt.de/studieren/studierende_tu/internationale_studierende_1/index.de.jsp

item.title: Post #348
🎓 From Campus to Career: International Alumni-Talk 💡
item.description:
🎓 From Campus to Career: International Alumni-Talk 💡
Are you an international student wondering how to successfully transition from university to the German job market?
Join our first International Alumni-Talk, build your own professional Network and navigate your path from STEM studies to industry and science.
 
Our speaker Dr.-Ing. Fares Ali, alumnus of TU Darmstadt and professional at Hitachi Energy, provides first-hand insights!
 
In this interactive alumni talk, you'll learn about:
✅ Career opportunities in Germany's STEM sector
✅ Navigating the German job market
✅ Building professional networks
✅ Application tips and personal career experiences
 
📅 24 June 2026
🕓 4:00 PM – 6:00 PM (CEST)
📍 Hybrid Event
 
The event is organized by the Future Engineering Career Programme (FEC), TU Darmstadt Alumni Management, and the Department of Mechanical Engineering at TU Darmstadt.
Register now and connect your studies with your future career!

eveeno.com/fec_international-alumni
